name: Chicken Tomatillo Sauce with Mole Chile
recipeIngredient: 5 ounces skinless boneless chicken breasts (1 fillet)
1/2 teaspoon lemon zest, minced or more
4 tablespoons Monterey Jack cheese (with crumbled jalapeno)
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast onion and garlic: Peel and trim the onion; slice the head of garlic to expose the cloves. Seal in an aluminum foil packet and spray with olive oil (optional); roast at 325°F for 45-60 minutes until aromatic.
2. Prepare chicken: Trim fat from chicken breasts, cut into strips along the grain, and season with black pepper and Mexican spice mix. Spray a wok lightly; heat to sear and slightly char the chicken. Add bell peppers and mushrooms.
3. Cook sauce: In the center of the hot wok, make a well and add the sauce ingredients in order listed. Stir until mole paste blends with sauce liquids.
4. Adjust sauce: Thin the sauce with water or broth as it simmers; taste and adjust lemon zest and/or pepper. Add chopped cilantro and spoon stew over hot cooked noodles.
5. Finish dish: Sprinkle with crumbled Jalapeno Jack cheese. Serve with zucchini steamed with orange zest and thyme, boiled potato wedges (well drained), and condiments.
6. Variation: Use domestic and wild mushrooms; thin sauce with strained soaking liquid or vegetable broth. Serve half cup of pintos as a side dish.
7. Pasta options: Fettuccine, spaghetti, short lasagna, or homestyle thick noodles.
8. Broth: Use 1/2 cup nonfat chicken broth (low sodium) and then switch to water to thin the sauce as it simmers.
